text:
Market today: Tech/chip names face mixed signals. While some global tech names are seen as resilient against AI disruption, the sector also faces new geopolitical cyber risk tied to Nvidia. Broader market sentiment could be affected by rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions.

Your holdings: SOXL (-23.52%) is flagged SELL and is a leveraged chip ETF, needing immediate attention. SPAL (-11.17%) is a new, leveraged ETF also requiring close monitoring. Remember, margin amplifies drops.

Watch: Keep an eye on the broader chip sector for any contagion from the cyber risk news. Also, LRCX and ASML are showing scanner BUY/ACCUMULATE signals if you're looking for new chip exposure.

Bottom line: Focus on managing risk in your leveraged, underperforming chip positions and stay alert to sector-specific news.
plays: SOXL is flagged SELL and down -23.52%. Given its leveraged nature and chip sector news, consider trimming your position or tightening its protective stop below current price.
SPAL is a new, leveraged ETF down -11.17%. Ensure your stop at 21.38 is respected to limit further drawdown.
If looking to add chip exposure, LRCX has a scanner BUY setup; consider entry 349.85-349.23 with a stop at 347.77.
